subject: Product Management
title: Willingness to Pay
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Willingness to Pay.

hook.text: Free users can vanish overnight. Paying users have made a commitment. That's why revenue is the ultimate PMF signal.

buildup.text: Willingness to pay means users value the product enough to exchange money — the strongest form of validation.

discovery.text: The Van Westendorp pricing model asks users four questions to find the acceptable price range and the optimal price.

twist.text: Many products with millions of free users fail to monetize. Usage without willingness to pay is weak PMF.

climax.text: The moment users willingly pay — and keep paying — is the clearest confirmation that you've built real value.

punchline.text: Free usage is interest. Payment is commitment.

quiz.question: Why is willingness to pay the strongest PMF signal?
options: Payment represents real commitment — users value the product enough to exchange money
Free products can never achieve PMF
